title: Java Operators Assignment
description: Practice assignment, increment, remainder, and compound operators.
learningGoals: Type compound assignment and increment operators.
Recognize remainder checks used in branch conditions.

completionChecks.explanation: count starts at 4, += 3 makes it 7, next reads 7 before count++ stores 8, and 8 % 2 == 0 is true, so it prints 7:true.

completionChecks.prompt: What does next hold after int next = count++; when count is 7?
choices: 8, because the increment is applied before the assignment
8, because next reads count again after the statement
7, because post-increment yields the value before incrementing
Compilation fails because count++ cannot appear in an assignment
completionChecks.explanation: Post-increment evaluates to the current value (7) and assigns that to next, then stores the incremented value (8) back into count.

completionChecks.prompt: What does count hold just before System.out.println(next + ":" + even); runs?
choices: Compilation fails because count++ cannot appear in an assignment
completionChecks.explanation: count += 3 changes count to 7, then the post-increment stores 7 in next and leaves count as 8 before even is calculated.
